learning_outcomes.description: Identify conservation issues and the key ecological, social and political factors involved with them.
Learn to critically analyse a conservation issue and present that information in concise writing
Apply science to solve real conservation problems.
Evaluate arguments about conservation issues, expressing them verbally and in written form.
Contribute in a professional manner to a team to achieve successful outcomes

assessments.task_description:
Based on the work carried out in the two practical sessions led by Professor Richard Fuller, you will complete a short written assessment. Full instructions will be given in class. Focus on quality and creativity in your answer. The prac work may be done in pairs or groups, but this assessment MUST be done individually.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Online submission by Turnitin only by the due date and time. Refer to Blackboard for the submission link. No hard copy or assignment cover sheets are required. Submission via email is not accepted.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
The two practical sessions led by Prof Salit Kark and tutors will aim to help students discuss and better understand the process of set priorities for invasive and native species management and biodiversity conservation, using a case study of issues related to management of native and introduced alien species. You will develop your own conservation and management plan for native and invasive species in Queensland and describe this in a written assessment in teams. The Prac work and this assessment will be undertaken in small groups, enhancing your team work skills, which are key in both conservation and management in the real world.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Online submission by Turnitin only by the due date and time. Refer to Blackboard for the submission link. No hard copy or assignment cover sheets are required. Submission via email is not accepted.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
The two practical sessions led by A/Prof Tatsuya Amano will focus on how we can systematically synthesise scientific evidence for conservaiton. You will learn how to use existing literature databases and search systems to conduct systematic literature searches and identify scientific evidence relevant to conservation in an unbiased and comprehensive manner. You will complete a short written assessment (less than 600 words) on developing, refining, and evaluating search strings for a systematic review, using the knowledge and approaches learned during the two sessions. Full instructions will be given in class. The prac work may be done in pairs or groups, but this assessment MUST be done individually.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Online submission by Turnitin only by the due date and time. Refer to Blackboard for the submission link. No hard copy or assignment cover sheets are required. Submission via email is not accepted.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
This task is completed under central examination conditions and schedules. It is the only individually verified, closed-book component of the course. The exam consists of ten short-answer questions that test your knowledge of the core concepts of conservation.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.
